description: Add a non-tenant-scoped appliance claim state record that stores token hash, expiry, and claim completion metadata.
Generate a cryptographically strong one-time appliance claim token during fresh appliance bootstrap.
Persist the raw appliance claim token in a Kubernetes Secret for operator retrieval.
Print a usable appliance claim URL during bootstrap output using the configured appliance app URL.
Persist only the hashed claim token in application state and never the raw token.
Ensure recover-mode bootstrap does not mint or expose a new claim token for an already-claimed appliance.
Add a dedicated `/auth/appliance-claim` route that is distinct from public registration and client portal setup.
Gate the appliance claim route so it only functions in appliance mode while the appliance is still unclaimed.
Add server-side token verification that checks hash match, expiry, unused state, and overall appliance unclaimed state.
Render an appliance claim form that collects full name, work email, organization name, password, and password confirmation.
Show explicit invalid-state UI for missing, invalid, expired, already-used, or already-claimed token scenarios.
Block claim completion if a first MSP admin already exists, even if a stale token is presented.
Create the minimum tenant row required for MSP auth/onboarding if no tenant exists yet during claim completion.
Create the tenant-scoped settings/bootstrap state required for `/msp` layout and onboarding checks to load after claim.
Create the first internal MSP user with local email/password credentials by reusing existing user creation and password hashing logic.
Assign the created first user to the MSP `Admin` role or equivalent existing highest-privilege default role.
Consume the claim token atomically with tenant/user creation so concurrent redemptions cannot create duplicate initial admins.
Establish a normal authenticated MSP session immediately after successful claim.
Redirect successful claim completion to `/msp/onboarding` instead of leaving the user on a terminal success page.
Disable first-user creation through the claim route after the appliance has been claimed once.
Document the operator retrieval flow for the claim token from the Kubernetes Secret in appliance docs/runbooks.
